text.tspan: Runtime path for a prerendered static site: the browser always hits CloudFront first,
then CloudFront serves from cache or fetches from S3.

div.style: height:50px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; text-align:center; color:#6C8294; font:600 14px Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.45;

foreignObject.div: HTML first, then CSS, JS, and images through the same CDN flow

g.text: Accept HTTPS request at the edge
location and run clean URL rewrite.

div.style: height:58px; color:#6C8294; font:600 14px Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.45;

foreignObject.div: Check whether prerendered HTML, CSS, JS, or images already exist in the edge cache.

div.style: height:40px; color:#6C8294; font:600 14px Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.45;

foreignObject.div: Only CloudFront can read these objects.

div.style: height:40px; color:#6C8294; font:600 14px Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.45;

foreignObject.div: Return the requested file back to CloudFront.

div.style: height:48px; color:#6C8294; font:600 15px Manrope, Arial, sans-serif; line-height:1.5;

foreignObject.div: 1. Browser requests a route or asset - 2. CloudFront receives the request and rewrites clean URLs. 3. Edge cache lookup - 4a. If hit, respond immediately - 4b. If miss, request S3. 5. S3 returns the prerendered file - 6. CloudFront caches the object and returns it.
